videos.title: AI’s Next Race: Cost, Control, and Compute
videos.description:
The AI race is shifting from who has the biggest model to who can run, control and deploy AI most effectively.

Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas joins to discuss the company’s new orchestrator model, why he’s building on open-source Chinese AI, and his argument that “token value per watt” may decide the next phase of competition.

Then, Benchmark general partner Peter Fenton and Ollama CEO Jeff Morgan discuss the rise of open models, why enterprises are increasingly running models they can download and control, and what Ollama’s growth says about where the AI ecosystem is heading.

Together, the conversations get at one big question: in AI’s next race, will the winners be defined less by model access and more by cost, control and compute?

videos.title: Andy Burnham Is the UK PM in Waiting, Says Opinion's Price
videos.description:
Andy Burnham's path to becoming the next UK prime minister looks certain after a vast majority of Labour MPs formally nominated him to be the next party leader. At the end of the first day of the party's leadership contest to replace Keir Starmer, 322 of 403 Labour members of Parliament voted for Burnham. Responding to the confirmation of the vote number, Burnham said he was "deeply grateful" for the support he's received from party colleagues, saying it "reflects a shared belief that Britain needs a new approach to politics." Bloomberg Opinion's Rosa Prince joins Caroline hepker and Rachel Evans on Daybreak Europe to discuss.'
--------

videos.title: Why Britain Sent Its Largest Aircraft Carrier to Hunt Russian Subs | WSJ
videos.description:
HMS PRINCE OF WALES – Amid growing Russian threats in the Arctic, the U.K. Royal Navy has deployed its flagship aircraft carrier to deter Moscow’s incursions into strategic waterways. 

As a sign of the tension in the area, in early July, a Russian plane flew close to the carrier and dropped a large number of sonar trackers in its path, before British F-35 jets scrambled to escort the plane away.  

WSJ’s Sune Rasmussen goes on board the ship to understand how NATO is working to confront Russia in the High North.

Chapters:
0:00 Russia’s NATO threats in the High North
1:18 On the flight deck 
2:19 Inside the threats to the region
5:39 Life on board the ship
7:16 Challenges defending the Arctic

WSJ Originals features in-depth reporting, investigations and on-the-ground journalism that uncovers the forces shaping business, politics and the world around us.

#Russia #NATO #WSJ

videos.title: Stocks Waver as SK Hynix Makes US Debut | Open Interest 7/10/2026
videos.description:
Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." SK Hynix makes Wall Street history with the biggest-ever U.S. debut by a foreign company. Delta beats earnings expectations, but shares fluctuate on a cloudy outlook. And earnings season ramps up next week, with Wall Street's biggest banks set to report another blockbuster quarter. Emily Roland of Manulife John Hancock Investments, John Stoltzfus of Oppenheimer Asset Management, and Di Zhou of Thornburg Investment Management weigh in as traders hold off on making big bets ahead of the weekend.

videos.title: Why Are Israeli Forces Destroying American Homes in Lebanon? | WSJ
videos.description:
When Israeli forces occupied the town of Bint Jbeil in southern Lebanon, thousands of miles away in Michigan, Danielle Elzayat thought to check on her house. 

Dearborn and neighboring Dearborn Heights are home to the largest Lebanese-American population in the U.S.; many families trace their roots back to Bint Jbeil, and, like Elzayat, still own property there. 

It became a daily ritual: Danielle Elzayat would pore over satellite images circulated by neighbors and family to find the house her father built—until one day, there was only rubble. 

WSJ analyzes satellite imagery and on-the-ground video to show how dozens of homes owned by Americans have been destroyed by Israel forces since April. 

Chapters:
0:00 U.S. homes destroyed by Israel
0:58 Emigration from Bint Jbeil
2:42 IDF mission in Lebanon
5:10 Bint Jbeil the symbol
6:31 Ongoing destruction

WSJ video investigations use visual evidence to reveal the truth behind the most important stories of the day.

#Israel #Lebanon #WSJ

videos.title: How AI Super PACs Are Shaping The Midterms
videos.description:
Members of Congress are debating the future of AI regulation at a pivotal moment for the technology. In an effort to influence the next class of lawmakers, dueling super PACs backed by AI giants like OpenAI and Anthropic are pouring millions of dollars into the midterm elections. CNBC’s Emily Wilkins breaks it down.

videos.title: Opening Doors for Women Entrepreneurs | Talking Development
videos.description:
What does it take to help women-owned small businesses start, grow, and thrive? In this episode of Talking Development, World Bank Managing Director Anna Bjerde speaks with Kelly Tullier, Vice Chair, Chief People and Corporate Affairs Officer at Visa and Chair of Visa Foundation, and Adesuwa Okunbo Rhodes, Founder and Managing Partner of Aruwa Capital Management. They discuss why women entrepreneurs need more than access to capital, how partnerships can unlock skills, trust, and scale, and what it will take to build a stronger ladder of opportunity for women-led businesses across developing economies.



ABOUT THE WORLD BANK 🌐 The World Bank is one of the world’s largest sources of funding and knowledge for low-income countries. Its five institutions share a commitment to reducing poverty, increasing shared prosperity, and promoting sustainable development on a livable planet. http://www.worldbank.org

videos.title: How Warsh's Task Forces Will Reshape The Federal Reserve
videos.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is floating major revisions to the central bank’s approach to assets, productivity, data, inflation and public communications. To achieve this, Warsh has created 5 "task forces" while soliciting interest from individuals outside of the Fed. This presents an opportunity for a small unelected group to influence the broader U.S. economy and value of U.S. dollars circulating globally. CNBC's Matt Peterson reports.

Credits
Reporter: Matt Peterson
Produced and Edited by: Carlos Waters
Camera by: Charlotte Morabito
Animation: Jason Reginato
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin
Additional Footage: Getty Images

videos.title: Palantir: profits, procurement and power | FT Film
videos.description:
Palantir is one of the world's most controversial technology companies. Its extraordinary growth was steered by a revolving door of executives moving between Palantir and the corridors of power in Washington and Westminster. It has won contracts with governments across the globe and some of the world's biggest companies but it's now under pressure from human rights activists, politicians and data specialists on both sides of the Atlantic. This FT film looks at the company's remarkable rise and examines what's next for Peter Thiel and Alex Karp's company

#palantir #technology #ai #artificialintelligence #siliconvalley #peterthiel #alexkarp #donaldtrump #surveillance #nhs #nationalhealthservice #police #fdp #federateddataplatform #ice #immigration #israel #gaza #ukraine #washington #westminster #us #uk #data #datasecurity #bigdata #newyork  #petermandelson #globalcounsel #politics #lobbying

videos.title: Why Americans Pay $170K for This British SUV | WSJ Coveted
videos.description:
Ever since Jaguar Land Rover ceased production of the classic Land Rover Defender, fans have been snapping up discontinued models and breathing new life into them. Now, the value of old Land Rovers is rocketing as a new generation of car enthusiasts falls in love with this simple off-roader.

WSJ explains why the classic “British Jeep” has surged in popularity, and why they can sell for up to $170,000 in the U.S.

Chapters:
0:00 The Land Rover’s abilities 
1:56 What makes this off-roader so quirky
3:18 The two-way relationship
4:32 How the Land Rover was born
6:01 Jaguar Land Rover ends production
7:40 An icon reborn
8:57 Why the U.S. market is different

WSJ's Coveted highlights the precision and process behind expertly-crafted cult favorites — from the pencils beloved by Disney animators to the sushi knives breaking thousands of years of Japanese tradition. These stories show how the smallest everyday items can be transformed into the ultimate luxury splurge.

#LandRover #Cars #WSJ

videos.title: Why Krispy Kreme Can’t Fully Automate Its Donuts | WSJ
videos.description:
Krispy Kreme is pushing its machinery to boost efficiency and reverse a period of missed sales targets and profit losses. The company is trying to boost efficiency with automation but found some tasks still require human hands. 

WSJ sits down with Krispy Kreme CEO Josh Charlesworth to discuss the company’s turnaround strategy.

Chapters:
0:00 Krispy Kreme’s stalled growth
1:11 Automation
2:29 The human touch
3:13 The growing challenges in the industry
5:37 What’s next during the GLP-1 era?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#KrispyKreme #Donuts #WSJ

videos.title: Can Industrial Policy Boost Growth and Job Creation in South Asia?
videos.description:
South Asia continues to be the world’s fastest-growing region. But amidst a challenging global environment, governments are increasingly using industrial policy to shape their economies and achieve development results. Join this conversation with World Bank Chief Economist, Indermit Gill, and World Bank Group Chief Economist for Asia, Franziska Ohnsorge, as they discuss how carefully designed policy measures can support sustainable growth and job creation across the region. 

Learn more: 

https://www.worldbank.org/en/region/sar/publication/south-asia-economic-update?cid=ECR_YT_Worldbank_EN_EXT 

00:00 Introduction
00:45 Repercussions of the conflict in the Middle East
06:45 Jobs agenda in South Asia
07:57 Industrial policy in South Asia
16:13 Trade reforms in India and Sri Lanka

videos.title: Who Is Funding Pres. Trump’s Freedom 250 Celebrations?
videos.description:
CNBC's Luke Fountain is at the Great American State Fair on the Washington Mall for celebrations of the nation's 250th Independence Day. Freedom 250, the sponsor of the state fair, has become politicized because the Trump administration has pushed many events through that, rather than America 250, a bipartisan group created by Congress. It’s raised concerns from ethics watchdogs and Democrats.

Reporter Luke Fountain
Produced, Shot and Edited by Carlos Waters
Production Assistant Irit Skulnik
Senior Director of Video Lindsey Jacobson
Additional Footage: Getty Images
Additional Sources: Natural Resources Committee Democrats, America250.org, Public Employees for Environmental Responsibility, National Park Foundation

videos.description:
American Express and Chase increasingly invest in cardholder perks, like lounges, at events and experiences around the world. Both companies have raised the fees on their premium credit cards as they seek to attract affluent cardholders and increase spending.

videos.title: ICE Could Sign A $220M Contract With A Taser Maker That Trump Invested Millions In
videos.description:
In February 2026, President Trump invested up to $5 million in top U.S. taser-maker Axon Enterprise. Two weeks later, ICE announced that it was seeking a $220 million contract to more than quadruple its inventory. Experts say that Axon is the only company able to meet the requirement outlined by ICE. CNBC’s Luke Fountain breaks it down.

Reporter: Luke Fountain
Produced by: Macklin Fishman
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Shot by: Charlotte Morabito

videos.title: Fireside Chat with Ajay Banga and Daniel Chapo, President of Mozambique | Fragility Forum 2026
videos.description:
This fireside chat brings together World Bank Group President Ajay Banga and Mozambique President Daniel Chapo to discuss fragility, conflict, and development.
The conversation highlights the World Bank Group’s FCV Strategy, with a focus on anticipating risks, creating jobs in fragile settings, and delivering practical solutions alongside governments.

videos.title: Fireside Chat with Ajay Banga | World Bank Group Youth Summit 2026
videos.description:
What will it take to build economies where young people don’t just survive - they lead?
What’s real, and what’s next?

World Bank Group President Ajay Banga sits down for a fireside chat at the 13th World Bank Group Youth Summit, bringing together young leaders (18–35) from around the world to tackle the issues shaping their future.

Under the theme hashtag Future Works: Designing Jobs for the Digital Age, the 2026 Summit spotlights three critical pillars of opportunity:
• Education and skills
• Entrepreneurship
• Agriculture

videos.title: Inside The Rise And Fall Of Kohl's
videos.description:
Kohl's rose to its peak as a department store in the 2000s, with a focus on a strong in-store experience, coupons and rewards. Now, after years of stagnant sales and a rough patch on Wall Street, Kohl's is trying to get back to what made it a household name.

videos.title: Why Analysts Say The Auto Industry Is Heading For Demographic Cliff
videos.description:
The auto industry is staring at a 'perfect storm' of slowing population growth, longer lasting cars, high prices, and new technology. These factors stand to put significant downward pressure on new car sales by 2040, according to analysts at Bain & Co.. The landscape could be far more competitive and prone to consolidation.

videos.title: What Countries Want From Multilateral Development Banks - Talking Development
videos.description:
What do countries actually want from multilateral development banks (MDBs) and where are they still falling short? In this episode of Talking Development, World Bank Managing Director Anna Bjerde speaks with Sara Pantuliano, Chief Executive of ODI Global, about survey findings from 650 government and MDB officials across 125 countries. The discussion explores why MDBs remain relevant, why countries still value their financing, knowledge, and convening power, and what needs to change to deliver faster, more tailored, and more effective support. 

They explore why MDB relevance is still growing, where countries say MDBs need to improve, why speed and project preparation matter, how technical assistance can better reflect local realities, what stronger country presence and coordination could change. Learn more: https://odi.org/en/publications/reforming-multilateral-development-banks-perspectives-from-client-countries/

If you work on development finance, policy reform, public sector delivery or multilateral cooperation, find practical insight into how countries view MDB performance today.

00:00 Introduction
00:25 What client countries actually think about MDBs
01:13 Why MDB relevance is still growing
02:03 Where MDBs are still falling short
03:01 Why countries may turn to market borrowing instead
04:02 Biggest areas for improvement
04:30 Country presence and tailored technical assistance
06:20 Faster project preparation for better results

videos.title: AI for Clean Air: Scaling Smarter Solutions
videos.description:
Air pollution is one of the most pressing development challenges globally, with far-reaching impacts on public health, economic growth, climate resilience, and sustainable development. In South Asia alone, nearly one billion people are exposed to hazardous air every day, leading to around one million premature deaths annually and shortening average life expectancy by more than three years.

This AI for Clean Air virtual event brought together global experts, innovators, and regional leaders to explore how artificial intelligence is transforming air quality management (AQM)—from real-time pollution mapping and predictive forecasting to private sector solutions and job creation.

Featuring perspectives from across South Asia and beyond, the discussion highlighted how AI-driven tools are moving from pilots to system-scale impact, showcased emerging partnerships, and examined policy pathways to scale solutions in complex and rapidly evolving environments.

Topics included artificial intelligence, air quality management, pollution forecasting, satellite monitoring, low-cost sensors, environmental governance, climate intelligence, public health, clean energy, and sustainable AI infrastructure.

Visit the event page: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/scaling-ai-solutions-to-tackle-air-pollution?cid=ECR_YT_worldbank_EN_EXT 

👤SPEAKERS
- Mike Bergin, PhD, Sternberg Family Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering, @dukeuniversity 
- Sarah Vogel, Senior Vice President, Healthy Communities, @EdfOrg 
- Dr Arunabha Ghosh, Founder-CEO, Council on Energy, Environment and Water (CEEW) 
- Shakriya Pandey, Founder-CEO of BOTS Industries, and Founder, VayuDrishti 
Moderator: Genevieve Connors, Manager, Environment, Policy & Regulations, @WorldBankGroup 

CHAPTERS
00:00 Introduction: AI and Air Pollution in South Asia
03:32 AI-Powered Air Quality Monitoring with Sensors and Satellite Data (Mike Bergin)
06:46 How Punjab Uses AI for Air Pollution Monitoring and Enforcement (Imran Hamid Sheikh)
10:30 Panel Discussion: AI for Air Quality Management and Public Health
12:17 How AI Decision Support Systems Improve Air Quality Management (Arunabha Ghosh)
18:08 Scaling Air Quality Innovation from Pilots to System-Wide Impact (Sarah Vogel)
25:22 How Nepal Uses AI, Satellites, and Sensors for Hyperlocal Forecasting (Shakriya Pandey)
30:38 Partnerships Between Governments, Researchers, and Startups (Arunabha Ghosh)
38:20 How AI Can Benefit Underserved Communities and Smaller Cities (Sarah Vogel)
45:16 Climate Intelligence, Job Creation, and Skills for the Future (Shakriya Pandey)
50:33 Can AI Be Sustainable? Energy Use, Data Centers, and Clean Energy (Sarah Vogel)
52:28 AI Collaboration and Resource Sharing for Environmental Solutions (Shakriya Pandey)
53:40 AI, Energy Security, and Climate Resilience: The Sustainability Challenge (Arunabha Ghosh)
57:20 Key Takeaways: The Future of AI for Clean Air and Public Health

videos.title: Inside Africa’s Largest Clean Cooking Factory | BURN in Kenya
videos.description:
What does the clean energy transition look like in practice? Join Aki Nishio, World Bank Vice President for Development Finance, on a visit to BURN’s factory in Kenya to see how clean cooking innovation is being scaled across Africa.

BURN is the world’s largest clean cooking company and a leading manufacturer of clean cookstoves, electric cookers, and energy-efficient cooking equipment in Africa. In this video, see how BURN is expanding production across Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, Tanzania, Malawi, and beyond—while helping to reduce indoor air pollution, improve health outcomes, and support economic growth.

Learn how IDA financing helped accelerate BURN’s growth, enabling the company to expand production capacity, strengthen manufacturing, and increase access to clean cooking solutions for households, schools, and public institutions—creating jobs and improving lives across the region.

0:00-0:24: What is BURN?
0:25-0:56: Innovation fueled by IDA
0:57-1:44: BURN's Impact
1:45-2:52: The process and testimonial
2:53-4:24: The potential for manufacturing
4:25-5:06: How this creates jobs

What do you think is the biggest opportunity for scaling clean cooking in Africa—finance, manufacturing, consumer access, or policy support?
#CleanCooking #AfricaManufacturing #Kenya #EnergyAccess #DevelopmentFinance

videos.title: How Can Water Solutions Feed 10 Billion People on a Livable Planet?
videos.description:
How can we feed a global population of 10 billion by 2050? This video explores how smarter water management can transform food systems. Drawing on the Nourish and Flourish report, it shows how aligning food production with water availability can boost yields, create jobs, improve livelihoods, and protect the environment. videos.title: Daron Acemoglu on the Good-Jobs Imperative — ABCDE 2026
videos.description:
The 2026 Annual Bank Conference on Development Economics (ABCDE) opens with a keynote on on the urgent need for good jobs across the world, featuring World Bank Group Chief Economist Indermit Gill and Nobel laureate Daron Acemoglu.

Over the next 25 years, countries across Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and the Middle East will see an unprecedented surge in their working-age population. This shift could become one of the defining economic opportunities of the century—or a source of growing instability.

World Bank Group Chief Economist Indermit Gill will set the stage for this important discussion, outlining the challenges and opportunities the world will face in creating productive and rewarding jobs for the next generation.

- Learn more: https://live.worldbank.org/en/event/2026/daron-acemoglu-on-the-good-jobs-imperative-abcde-2026
